sourceMovie.title: Saw VI
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Saw VI works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The horror and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Saw III
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Saw VI clicked for you, Saw III (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Saw VI?
faq.answer: Try Saw III, Red Dragon, The Hunt—each shares DNA with Saw VI while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Saw VI?
faq.answer: Saw VI (2009) is best described as Horror, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Saw VI part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Saw Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
